senses.definition: In botany, the state or process of a plant part being twisted so it appears upside down.
examples: The orchid's resupinations ensure that its flowers face downward.
Researchers noted several resupinations in the developing blossoms.

senses.definition: Lying flat on your back, face upward.
examples: The patient lay resupine on the examination table.
He fell backward and remained resupine on the ground.

senses.definition: Gave someone or something new supplies again after they had run low or run out.
examples: The camp was resupplied by helicopter after the storm.
They resupplied the ship with food and fuel.

senses.definition: Gives or sends new supplies to someone or something again.
examples: The charity resupplies the clinic every month.
A support ship resupplies the research station during winter.

senses.definition: New supplies that are sent again to a person, place, or group.
examples: The soldiers waited for resupplies from headquarters.
Additional resupplies will arrive in the morning.

senses.definition: To provide someone or something with new supplies again.
examples: They had to resupply the hikers halfway through the trek.
The base is resupplied by air twice a week.

senses.definition: The act of giving new supplies again, or the supplies that are given.
examples: The team is waiting for the next resupply.
A resupply of fresh water is due tomorrow.

senses.definition: Giving or sending new supplies again to someone or something.
examples: They are resupplying the remote village by boat.
Resupplying the expedition in winter is difficult.

senses.definition: To support something again after previous support stopped or failed.
examples: The company decided to resupport the old software for another year.
They agreed to resupport the project with additional funding.

senses.definition: To suppose or assume something again, often after reconsidering it.
examples: If we resuppose that demand will grow, the numbers look different.
The theory resupposes an earlier idea about human behavior.

senses.definition: The act of supposing again or an assumption that is taken up once more.
examples: The resupposition of this claim changes the whole argument.
His analysis depends on the resupposition of earlier hypotheses.

senses.definition: To suppress something again, such as a feeling, activity, or reaction.
examples: She tried to resuppress her anger during the meeting.
The government moved to resuppress the protests.

senses.definition: The act of suppressing something again after it has returned.
examples: The resuppression of the uprising was swift and severe.
Therapy helped him avoid the simple resuppression of his feelings.

senses.definition: To come back to the surface of water or the ground after being under it.
examples: The divers agreed to resurface after twenty minutes.
After the storm, several submerged rocks resurfaced near the shore.

senses.definition: To appear or happen again after a period of being absent or forgotten.
examples: Old arguments began to resurface during the negotiations.
The issue is likely to resurface before the next election.

senses.definition: To put a new surface on a road, path, or other structure.
examples: The city plans to resurface the main highway this summer.
They resurfaced the tennis courts to improve safety.

senses.definition: Came back to the surface of water or the ground after being under it.
examples: The submarine resurfaced near the coast.
The diver resurfaced to adjust his equipment.

senses.definition: Appeared again or became important again after being absent or forgotten.
examples: Old fears resurfaced as the deadline approached.
The controversy resurfaced in the media years later.

senses.definition: Had a new surface put on a road, path, or other structure.
examples: The parking lot was resurfaced last year.
They resurfaced the bridge to handle heavier traffic.

senses.definition: A person or machine that puts a new surface on something such as a road, floor, or ice rink.
examples: The ice resurfacer smoothed the rink between periods.
They rented a concrete resurfacer for the driveway project.

senses.definition: People or machines that put new surfaces on things such as roads, floors, or ice rinks.
examples: Several resurfacers worked overnight to finish the road repairs.
Modern ice resurfacers are much quieter than older models.

senses.definition: Comes back to the surface of water or the ground after being under it.
examples: The whale resurfaces every few minutes to breathe.
The submarine resurfaces at night.

senses.definition: Appears or becomes important again after being absent or forgotten.
examples: The question of funding resurfaces every year.
Her childhood memories resurface when she visits the town.

senses.definition: Puts a new surface on a road, path, or similar structure.
examples: The city regularly resurfaces its main roads.
The council resurfaces the playground every few years.

senses.definition: The process of putting a new surface on a road, path, or similar structure.
examples: Road resurfacing will cause delays this week.
The pool needs resurfacing before summer.

senses.definition: The action of appearing again, especially of thoughts, feelings, or issues.
examples: The resurfacing of old conflicts worried the team.
Therapy led to the resurfacing of buried memories.

senses.definition: A literary or religious word meaning "I shall rise again."
examples: The gravestone was simply inscribed with "Resurgam."
The motto "Resurgam" expressed their hope after defeat.

senses.definition: To rise or become active, strong, or popular again after a period of decline.
examples: Interest in the band began to resurge after the documentary.
The city's economy is expected to resurge next year.

senses.definition: Rose or became active, strong, or popular again after a period of decline.
examples: The company resurged after a series of smart investments.
Traditional crafts have resurged in recent years.

senses.definition: A new growth, increase, or interest in something after it has been quiet or less active for some time.
examples: There has been a resurgence of interest in vinyl records.
The city is experiencing a resurgence in tourism.

senses.definition: Instances of new growth, increase, or interest in something after a quiet or weak period.
examples: The report described several resurgences of the disease.
We have seen resurgences in demand after each marketing campaign.

senses.definition: The state or quality of rising or becoming strong again.
examples: Analysts spoke of a resurgency in the regional economy.
The resurgency of local culture has surprised many observers.

senses.definition: Growing, becoming strong, or becoming successful again after a period of decline.
examples: The resurgent economy created thousands of new jobs.
Resurgent nationalism has changed the political landscape.

senses.definition: Rises or becomes active, strong, or popular again after a period of decline.
examples: Consumer confidence resurges when unemployment falls.
Violence often resurges after peace talks fail.

senses.definition: Rising or becoming active, strong, or popular again.
examples: The report examined the country's resurging industries.
Resurging interest in the language has led to new courses.

senses.definition: To surprise someone again.
examples: The unexpected twist managed to resurprise the audience.
She tried to resurprise him with another party the following year.

senses.definition: To bring someone back to life, especially in a religious or imaginary context.
examples: The story says a powerful spell can resurrect the hero.
Some religions teach that the dead will be resurrected.

senses.definition: To make something active, popular, or successful again after it has ended or been forgotten.
examples: They plan to resurrect the old festival.
The director resurrected his career with a hit film.

senses.definition: Brought someone back to life, especially in a religious or imaginary context.
examples: According to the story, the wizard resurrected the fallen king.
They believed their god had resurrected him.

senses.definition: Made something active, popular, or successful again after it had ended or been forgotten.
examples: The band resurrected its classic sound on the new album.
The council resurrected an old plan for the park.

senses.definition: Able to be brought back to life or brought back into use or activity.
examples: The character is not resurrectible within the rules of the game.
Some traditions see the body as fully resurrectible.

senses.definition: Bringing someone back to life, or bringing something back into use, popularity, or success.
examples: The legend tells of a healer resurrecting the dead.
They are resurrecting an old community project.

senses.definition: In some religions, the act of a dead person coming back to life, especially at the end of the world.
examples: Many Christians believe in the resurrection of the dead.
The painting shows the resurrection of Christ.

senses.definition: The act of bringing something back into use, activity, or popularity after it has ended or been forgotten.
examples: The town has seen a resurrection of its music scene.
The film led to a resurrection of interest in the author.

senses.definition: Relating to or involving resurrection, especially in a religious sense.
examples: The hymn has a strong resurrectional theme.
Resurrectional imagery appears throughout the poem.

senses.definition: Relating to resurrection or to causing something to rise or be active again.
examples: The movement had a strong resurrectionary message.
He described the reforms as resurrectionary for the economy.

senses.definition: A person who illegally dug up dead bodies in the past to sell them for medical study.
examples: In the nineteenth century, resurrectioners supplied bodies to anatomy schools.
Stories of resurrectioners frightened local communities.

senses.definition: The act of digging up dead bodies for illegal sale or study, especially in the past.
examples: Resurrectioning was driven by the need for medical cadavers.
Laws were passed to stop resurrectioning.

senses.definition: To treat someone as if they were a body to be dug up, or to dig up a body for study, especially in the past.
examples: The grave was resurrectionised by body snatchers.
He was accused of attempting to resurrectionise the newly buried corpse.

senses.definition: The practice of illegally digging up dead bodies to sell them for medical study, especially in the past.
examples: Resurrectionism flourished when demand for cadavers was high.
New laws were introduced to combat resurrectionism.

senses.definition: A person who illegally dug up dead bodies to sell them for medical study, especially in the past.
examples: The resurrectionist worked at night to avoid being seen.
Several resurrectionists were arrested near the cemetery.

senses.definition: People who illegally dug up dead bodies to sell them for medical study, especially in the past.
examples: Resurrectionists supplied bodies to surgeons and medical schools.
The public demanded stronger punishments for resurrectionists.

senses.definition: Events in which someone is brought back to life, or situations in which something becomes active or popular again.
examples: Stories about miraculous resurrections are common in folklore.
The article described several resurrections of the brand over the decades.

senses.definition: Having the power or effect of bringing something back to life or back into use or activity.
examples: The policy had a resurrective effect on local businesses.
He spoke of the poem's resurrective power after tragedy.

senses.definition: A person or thing that brings someone or something back to life, or back into use or success.
examples: The media called her the resurrector of the fashion house.
In the story, a mysterious resurrector returns the hero to life.

senses.definition: People or things that bring someone or something back to life, or back into use or success.
examples: Historians see them as resurrectors of traditional music.
Such policies can be powerful resurrectors of local economies.

senses.definition: Brings someone back to life, especially in a religious or imaginary context.
examples: The ritual supposedly resurrects the ancient king.
In the story, magic resurrects the fallen warrior.

senses.definition: Makes something active, popular, or successful again after it has ended or been forgotten.
examples: The new campaign resurrects a classic slogan.
This decision resurrects hopes for a deal.

senses.definition: To surrender again, especially after having already given up once.
examples: The army was forced to resurrender after breaking the agreement.
He vowed never to resurrender his rights.

senses.definition: To surround someone or something again.
examples: Security forces moved in to resurround the building.
They quickly resurrounded the camp after the escape.

senses.definition: To measure or examine an area, situation, or group again.
examples: Engineers will resurvey the land before building starts.
The team decided to resurvey customers after the changes.

senses.definition: An act of surveying something again.
examples: A resurvey of the border was ordered.
The resurvey confirmed the original results.

senses.definition: Measured or examined an area, situation, or group again.
examples: The coastline was resurveyed after the earthquake.
They resurveyed the patients two years later.
